text: The Student Code of Conduct outlines the standard of behaviour to which students at Queen’s University are held. It defines expectations for student conduct within the university community.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Queen’s University recognizes that all members of the university community have the right to be free from harassment and discrimination. This includes sexual harassment; harassment based on gender, race, ethnicity, religion, creed; and harassment based on sexual orientation or analogous grounds.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The Sexual Violence Prevention and Response Policy affirms Queen’s University’s commitment to maintaining a positive learning, living, and working environment in which sexual violence will not be tolerated. The policy outlines institutional responsibilities for prevention, response, and support.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Queen’s University is strongly committed to the principles of Equity, Diversity, Inclusion, and Indigeneity (EDII). The university seeks to create a just, equitable, and sustainable society by fostering belonging, protecting human rights and dignity, and supporting inclusive excellence in graduate education.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Academic Accommodations for Students with a Disability require that university administration, faculty, staff, and students support individualized and appropriate accommodation plans to the point of undue hardship. Accommodations must preserve academic standards and adhere to principles of academic integrity.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The Short-Term Academic Considerations protocol outlines procedures for graduate students to request academic consideration in extenuating circumstances, in alignment with the Queen’s Senate Policy on Academic Consideration for Students in Extenuating Circumstances (approved April 2017).

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Academic Integrity represents a commitment to honesty, trust, fairness, respect, responsibility, and the courage to act on these values in all academic circumstances.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Research Integrity reflects a commitment to conduct research honestly, openly, and fairly, and to report suspected instances of misconduct. Research integrity policies apply to all university research except course-based research, which is governed by academic integrity policies.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Research Ethics concerns principles and practices that balance the protection of research participants with the legitimate goals of research. Resources and CORE training courses are available to support ethical research conduct.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Indigenous Research Ethics emphasizes the importance of building ethical relationships and research partnerships with Indigenous communities. Researchers are encouraged to learn community research priorities and collaboratively design projects from the outset. CORE Module 9 addresses research involving First Nations, Inuit, and Métis Peoples of Canada.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Intellectual Property refers to results of intellectual or artistic activity that can be owned, including inventions, publications, computer software, works of art, industrial and artistic designs, and other creations protected under patent, copyright, or trademark laws.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The Guide to Graduate Supervision Handbook provides guidelines to foster productive working relationships between supervisors and graduate students, outlining roles, responsibilities, and expectations.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Graduate students may request and receive a leave of absence without prejudice to their academic standing for various reasons, including maternity and parental leave, medical reasons, compassionate grounds, or gender affirmation. The leave of absence policy outlines eligibility and procedures.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs
